rag_rules: Place AGREE assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes when they refer to visible ports and features.
Use implementation annexes for bindings that depend on subcomponent connections or implementation details.
generic_rules: Ensure all AGREE identifiers are declared or visible in the AADL model scope.
Do not introduce package-qualified names or types not present in the input AADL model.
Place input range assumptions and output envelope guarantees in component type contracts when bounds are explicitly stated.
Avoid using implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Constants and equations in AGREE should use valid AADL data types and feature names.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type annexes unless they are valid in that scope.
Do not assume numeric bounds for ports unless explicitly stated in the requirement.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE validator diagnostics and repair patterns
Model analyst extraction of AADL structure
AGREE annex syntax and assume/guarantee structure
